name: Vegetable Ladder Loaf
recipeIngredient: 2 medium red bell pepper, or yellow or orange
1/4 cup toasted pine nuts, or slivered almonds
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast the peppers until skin is charred, then remove the skin. Cut into strips.
2. In a food processor, combine spinach, basil, Parmesan cheese, and garlic. Pulse until well combined. Add 1 tablespoon of the beaten egg and process until nearly smooth.
3. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or plain brown paper. Unfold sheets of pastry on each baking sheet. Place one sheet of pastry on a baking sheet and brush lightly with some of the beaten egg. Sprinkle half of the bread crumbs in a 3-inch strip down the center of the pastry. Spread half of the spinach-basil mixture on top, within one inch of the ends. Sprinkle with half of the nuts. Arrange half of the pepper strips evenly over the filling.
4. On both sides of the filling, make 3-inch cuts from the edges toward the center at 1-inch intervals. Starting at one end, alternately fold opposite strips of dough at an angle across the filling.
5. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
6. Brush each pastry sheet with beaten egg.
7. Bake at 425°F for 25 minutes or until puffed and golden brown. Serve warm. Cut each pastry crosswise into six slices, then each slice in half to make 24 pieces.
